questions.question: In a right triangle, the side opposite a 35° angle is 7 cm and the hypotenuse is 12 cm. Which trigonometric ratio would you use to verify this relationship?
questions.explanation: The sine ratio relates the opposite side to the hypotenuse: sin(θ) = opposite/hypotenuse. Here, sin(35°) = 7/12.

questions.question: Find cos(60°) using the unit circle.
questions.explanation: On the unit circle, the point at 60° is (1/2, √3/2). The x-coordinate gives cosine, so cos(60°) = 1/2.

questions.question: A ladder leans against a wall at a 70° angle from the ground. If the ladder is 10 feet long, how far up the wall does it reach? Round to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: The height is the side opposite the 70° angle. Use sin(70°) = height/10, so height = 10·sin(70°) ≈ 10(0.94) = 9.4 feet.

questions.question: What is the value of tan(45°)?
questions.explanation: At 45°, the unit circle point is (√2/2, √2/2). Since tan(θ) = sin(θ)/cos(θ), we get tan(45°) = (√2/2)/(√2/2) = 1.

questions.question: In a right triangle, the adjacent side to a 40° angle is 8 inches. Find the hypotenuse length to the nearest tenth.
questions.explanation: Use the cosine ratio: cos(40°) = adjacent/hypotenuse = 8/hypotenuse. So hypotenuse = 8/cos(40°) ≈ 8/0.766 ≈ 10.4 inches.
